However, my rationale slightly differs; instead of solely relying on the Class II relationship which may vary among individuals, I emphasize the inherent design features of these teeth—namely, their narrow crowns and sharp edges—which create a more pronounced gap compared to adjacent teeth. Additionally, this pair often exhibits less wear over time, preserving the natural spaces better. 

Therefore, based on these points, I support **C**: Between upper canine and premolar.
